person.name: Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay

nameForms.fullText: Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Sir William de la Haye, 1st Lord Hay was the son of Sir Thomas de la Haye, 7th of Erroll and Elizabeth Stewart.

Sir William Hay of Erroll, Constable of Scotland, obtained from his uncle King Robert III, an engagement not to ratify or approve of any alienations to be made by his father without consent of the said William and of the King's council dated 19 March 1392/3.

William succeeded his father in 1406, who died shortly before 03 August 1406.

William was knighted at His Majesty's coronation, appointed one of the Wardens of the Marches in 1430.

He was invested as a Knight. He was Commissioner to treat for the ransom of King James I in 1423.2 He held the office of Joint Lord Warden of the Marches in 1430.

He was created 1st Lord Hay before March 1429/30, following an Act of 1428 drawing a distinction between Lords of Parliament and the ordinary lairds in the Scots baronage.

He died 'in crastino pentecostis' 1436.

He married Margaret Gray, the daughter of Sir Patrick Gray of Broxmouth.

«b»Children of Sir William de la Haye, 1st Lord Hay and Margaret Gray:«/b»
1.) Gilbert, who succeeded; d. 7 Sep 1436

2.) William of Ury in Kinkardineshire, charter from his father in the barony of Cowie 20 July 1430; d. shortly before 02 Feb 1471/2.

3.) Walter, a witness with brothers David & Thomas 14 Mar 1470/1.

4.) David

5.) Thomas

«b»Birth«/b»
Date: ABT 1374 Errol, Perthshire, Scotland 

«b»Note«/b»
"The family of Hay is one of the most illustrious in Scotland. Nearly seven centuries ago, William De Haya, settled in Lothian and filled office of Royal Butler during the reigns of Malcolm IV and William the Lion. He died about the year 1170 and left his wife Juliana, dau. Ranalpulph de Soulis, Lord of Liddesdale and his two sons: William, progenitor of the Earls of Errol and Robert, progenitor of the Earls of Locherwort, who for some generations were lords of Yester and are now Marquises of Tweeddale. Sir John de Haya, grandson of Robert, Married Margaret, dau. and heiress of Robert de Lyne, Lord of Locherwort and left a son. Sir Gilbert de Haya, who married Mary, dau. and heiress of Sir Simon Fraser of Oliver Castle and whose great-grandson was Sir Thomas Hay, Lord of Locherwort, married Joanna, dau. and heiress of Sir Hew Gifford, Lord of Yester, was celebrated in Scottish history for his necromantic powers ( see Scott's Marmion). Their son was Sir William Hay, Lord of Locherwort and Yester who married Alicia, dau. of Sir Gilbert Hay, Earl of Errol and left two sons. 1. Sir David Hay, Baron of Yester ( who was the ancestor of the Marquis of Tweedsdale) married Mary, dau. of 1st Earl of Angus, and the Princess Mary daughter of King Robert. Sir Edmond Hay, Lord of Tallo and Limplum was the ancestor of the Hays of Arran. Sir David Hay was succeeded by his son. who was John Hay, Baron of Yester, who by solemn investiture in Parliament, Jan 29,1487, was advanced to the dignity of Lord Hay of Yester. His grandson was John, third Lord Hay of Yester."

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay於大約1374年出生在英國蘇格蘭培爾培斯時，他的父親Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll33歲，母親Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland29歲。他於1390年之前與Margaret Gray結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1436年在英國蘇格蘭阿爾迪史爾圖利福享年63歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Haye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Helmut, Willi.

North German, East Frisian, and Dutch: from a personal name, Frisian Hajo, ancient Germanic Haio; or from a short form of the female name Hadewig, composed of the elements hadu ‘battle, strife’ + wīg ‘war’.
German: variant of   Hey   4, 5.

variants.name: Deshayes
meanings: French: topographic name for someone who lived by an enclosure, from the plural of Old French haye ‘hedge’ (see Haye and Hayes ), with fused preposition and definite article des ‘from the’; or a habitational name denoting someone from Les Hayes, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare Deshaies .

variants.name: Hayenga
meanings: East Frisian: patronymic from the personal name Hajo (see Haye 1). It is formed with the suffix -enga, a variant of -inga (from the patronymic suffix -ing and genitive plural ending -a).

variants.name: Hey
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of   Hay  .
Dutch: variant, mostly Americanized (or archaic) and Flemish, of Heij, a topographic name for someone who lived on a heath, Dutch hei, heide.
German: metonymic occupational name for a grower or mower of grass, from Middle High German höu ‘grass, hay’.

variants.name: Hayen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphonse.  German Heinz, Wilhelm.
Flemish, East Frisian, and North German: patronymic from   Haye   1.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay was born about 1374, in Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll, was 33 and his mother, Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland, was 29. He married Margaret Gray before 1390. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died in 1436, in Turriff, Aberdeenshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 63.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay est né environ 1374, à Perth, Perthshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Gray avant 1390. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1436, à Turriff, Aberdeenshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 63 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay um 1374 in Perth, Perthshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll 33 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland 29. Er hat vor 1390 Margaret Gray geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1436 im Alter von 63 Jahren in Turriff, Aberdeenshire, Schottland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay è nato il circa 1374 nel Perth, Perthshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Margaret Gray prima del 1390. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1436, nel Turriff, Aberdeenshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 63 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hayが約1374年にイギリススコットランドペルスシュアイアペルスで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Errollは33歳，母Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotlandは29歳であった。1390年以前にMargaret Grayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1436年にイギリススコットランドアバーディーンシュアイアトゥリッフで63歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay(이)가 대략 1374년 영국 스코틀랜드 퍼스실르 퍼스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1390년 이전 Margaret Gray(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1436년 영국 스코틀랜드 압르디실르 터리프에서 63세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay nasceu aproximadamente 1374, em Perth, Perthshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Gray antes 1390. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1436, em Turriff, Aberdeenshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 63 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay родился около 1374 г. в местности пирт, пиртшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Gray перед 1390 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1436 г. в местности туррифф, абирдиншир, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 63 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay nació aproximadamente 1374, en Perth, Perthshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Thomas de la Haye 7th Lord of Erroll, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Stewart Princess of Scotland, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Margaret Gray antes de 1390. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió en 1436, en Turriff, Aberdeenshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 63 años.
